en_definition: continuing firmly despite difficulty or opposition
example1: Her persistent efforts finally paid off with a better grade.
example1_ru: Её настойчивые усилия наконец принесли результат в виде лучшей оценки.
example2: A persistent cough kept her home from lectures all week.

en_definition: good at finding quick and clever ways to solve problems
example1: A resourceful student found extra materials online for the project.
example1_ru: Находчивый студент нашёл дополнительные материалы для проекта в интернете.
example2: The team was resourceful when the equipment broke down.

en_definition: careful to do things correctly and thoroughly
example1: A conscientious student always double-checks her homework before submitting it.
example1_ru: Добросовестная студентка всегда перепроверяет свою домашнюю работу перед сдачей.
example2: He is known for being conscientious about deadlines.

en_definition: able to change to suit new conditions or situations
example1: International students need to be adaptable to a new culture.
example1_ru: Иностранным студентам нужно уметь приспосабливаться к новой культуре.
example2: The software is adaptable to different operating systems.

en_definition: confident and direct in expressing opinions or needs
example1: Being assertive helps you speak up during group discussions.
example1_ru: Уверенность в себе помогает высказываться во время групповых обсуждений.
example2: She gave an assertive answer to the panel's difficult question.

en_definition: not talking too much about one's own abilities or achievements
example1: Despite winning the award, he remained modest about his success.
example1_ru: Несмотря на выигранную награду, он оставался скромным по поводу своего успеха.
example2: She gave a modest description of her research achievements.

en_definition: having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance
example1: His arrogant attitude made group work difficult for the whole team.
example1_ru: Его высокомерное отношение усложняло групповую работу для всей команды.
example2: Being arrogant about your grades will not win you friends.

en_definition: thinking about the needs and feelings of other people
example1: A considerate roommate keeps noise down during study hours.
example1_ru: Заботливый сосед по комнате не шумит во время учебных часов.
example2: It was considerate of her to share her notes with the class.

en_definition: careful not to upset or offend others when speaking
example1: The teacher gave tactful feedback on the weaker essay.
example1_ru: Учитель дал тактичный отзыв о слабом эссе.
example2: Be tactful when pointing out a colleague's mistake.

en_definition: expressing opinions honestly and directly, even if controversial
example1: She is outspoken about the need for more funding in research.
example1_ru: Она открыто высказывается о необходимости увеличения финансирования исследований.
example2: His outspoken comments sparked a heated debate in the seminar.

en_definition: having no more physical or mental energy left
example1: She felt completely drained after the three-hour exam.
example1_ru: Она чувствовала себя полностью обессиленной после трёхчасового экзамена.
example2: A short walk can help when you feel mentally drained.

en_definition: anxious or fearful about something that might happen
example1: He was apprehensive about presenting in front of the whole class.
example1_ru: Он тревожился о выступлении перед всем классом.
example2: Many students feel apprehensive before their first exam abroad.

en_definition: no longer feeling worried after receiving comfort or information
example1: She felt reassured after the tutor explained the exam format.
example1_ru: Она успокоилась после того, как преподаватель объяснил формат экзамена.
example2: His calm explanation left the students reassured.

en_definition: confused about where you are or what is happening
example1: New students often feel disoriented during their first week on campus.
example1_ru: Новые студенты часто чувствуют себя растерянными в первую неделю в кампусе.
example2: The sudden change in schedule left everyone disoriented.

en_definition: feeling angry because something seems unfair
example1: He felt resentful about being excluded from the group project.
example1_ru: Он чувствовал обиду из-за того, что его исключили из группового проекта.
example2: Some students were resentful of the sudden rule change.

en_definition: satisfied and at ease with a situation
example1: She seemed content with her final grade for the semester.
example1_ru: Она казалась довольной своей итоговой оценкой за семестр.
example2: He is content to work quietly in the library all afternoon.

en_definition: unable to stay still or relaxed because of nervous energy
example1: The students grew restless during the long lecture.
example1_ru: Студенты стали беспокойными во время долгой лекции.
example2: She felt restless the night before her final exam.

en_definition: having no particular interest or concern about something
example1: He remained indifferent to the outcome of the debate.
example1_ru: Он оставался равнодушным к исходу дебатов.
example2: Some voters seem indifferent to the election results.

en_definition: not easily convinced; having doubts about something
example1: The professor was skeptical about the new theory at first.
example1_ru: Профессор сначала скептически относился к новой теории.
example2: Reviewers remained skeptical until they saw the full data.

en_definition: hopeful and confident about the future
example1: She feels optimistic about passing the final exam.
example1_ru: Она настроена оптимистично насчёт сдачи выпускного экзамена.
example2: The team is optimistic that the project will succeed.

en_definition: expecting bad things to happen; lacking hope
example1: He felt pessimistic about his chances after the difficult exam.
example1_ru: После сложного экзамена он настроился пессимистично насчёт своих шансов.
example2: Try not to be too pessimistic before you see the results.

ru_translation: в растерянности, не знать что делать
en_definition: confused and unsure what to do or say
example1: I was at a loss when the professor asked such a difficult question.
example1_ru: Я был в растерянности, когда профессор задал такой сложный вопрос.
example2: She was at a loss for words after hearing the news.

en_definition: nervous, tense, or irritable
example1: Everyone was on edge before the results were announced.
example1_ru: Все были на нервах перед объявлением результатов.
example2: The noise outside kept him on edge all evening.

en_definition: extremely tired from excessive work or stress over a long time
example1: By the end of exam season, many students feel burnt out.
example1_ru: К концу сессии многие студенты чувствуют эмоциональное выгорание.
example2: Taking regular breaks helps prevent getting burnt out.

en_definition: to show understanding of someone's problem or feeling
example1: I sympathize with anyone who has to retake this course.
example1_ru: Я сочувствую всем, кому приходится пересдавать этот курс.
example2: She sympathized with her classmate's difficult situation.

en_definition: the ability to understand and share another person's feelings
example1: Good doctors show empathy toward their patients during every consultation.
example1_ru: Хорошие врачи проявляют эмпатию к своим пациентам во время каждой консультации.
example2: The workshop teaches empathy as a key communication skill.

en_definition: to say or do something to remove someone's doubts or fears
example1: The advisor reassured students that the exam would not be too hard.
example1_ru: Куратор заверил студентов, что экзамен не будет слишком сложным.
example2: She reassured her friend that everything would be fine.

en_definition: to make someone slightly angry or annoyed
example1: The constant noise irritated everyone in the reading room.
example1_ru: Постоянный шум раздражал всех в читальном зале.
example2: It irritates her when people are late for meetings.

en_definition: to comfort someone who is upset or disappointed
example1: Her friends tried to console her after the disappointing result.
example1_ru: Друзья пытались утешить её после разочаровывающего результата.
example2: He consoled his roommate after the failed presentation.

en_definition: to accept a difficult situation after a period of adjustment
example1: It took her a while to come to terms with the failed exam.
example1_ru: Ей потребовалось время, чтобы смириться с проваленным экзаменом.
example2: He finally came to terms with changing his major.

en_definition: to formally inform an employer that you are leaving a job
example1: She handed in her notice to focus on her master's degree.
example1_ru: Она подала заявление об уходе, чтобы сосредоточиться на магистратуре.
example2: He handed in his notice after accepting a research position.

en_definition: a temporary work position that gives practical experience, often for students
example1: She completed a summer internship at a technology company.
example1_ru: Она прошла летнюю стажировку в технологической компании.
example2: The internship gave him valuable hands-on experience.

en_definition: the activity of meeting people who might be useful to know professionally
example1: Networking at the career fair led to a summer job offer.
example1_ru: Нетворкинг на ярмарке вакансий привёл к предложению летней работы.
example2: Effective networking can open doors after graduation.

en_definition: a letter sent with a resume explaining why you are suitable for a job
example1: Write a clear cover letter explaining your motivation for the role.
example1_ru: Напишите чёткое сопроводительное письмо, объясняющее вашу мотивацию для этой должности.
example2: A strong cover letter can help your application stand out.

en_definition: a formal invitation to work for a company
example1: She received a job offer before finishing her final exams.
example1_ru: Она получила предложение о работе ещё до сдачи финальных экзаменов.
example2: He accepted the job offer after considering the salary and location.

en_definition: a trial period at the start of a job to assess suitability
example1: New employees usually have a three-month probation period.
example1_ru: У новых сотрудников обычно трёхмесячный испытательный срок.
example2: She passed her probation period with excellent reviews.

en_definition: the amount of work that a person or system has to do
example1: The workload increases significantly during exam season across every department.
example1_ru: Рабочая нагрузка значительно увеличивается в период сессии на всех кафедрах.
example2: Balancing coursework and a part-time job creates a heavy workload.

en_definition: additional time granted to complete a task past the original due date
example1: She requested a deadline extension because of illness.
example1_ru: Она попросила продлить срок сдачи из-за болезни.
example2: A deadline extension was granted for the whole class.

en_definition: the ability to use time effectively and productively
example1: Good time management helps you balance study and free time.
example1_ru: Хороший тайм-менеджмент помогает совмещать учёбу и свободное время.
example2: The workshop offers tips on time management for busy students.

en_definition: to decide which tasks are most important and deal with them first
example1: Prioritize your assignments based on their deadlines and difficulty level.
example1_ru: Расставляйте приоритеты по заданиям в зависимости от сроков сдачи и сложности.
example2: It is important to prioritize sleep during exam week.

en_definition: to do more than one task at the same time
example1: Trying to multitask during a lecture can reduce your focus.
example1_ru: Попытки делать несколько дел одновременно во время лекции могут снизить концентрацию.
example2: She learned to multitask between her job and her studies.

en_definition: the ability to control your own behavior and stick to a plan
example1: Online courses require a lot of self-discipline to complete.
example1_ru: Онлайн-курсы требуют большой самодисциплины для завершения.
example2: Self-discipline helped him study consistently every day.

en_definition: the length of time a person can focus on something without losing attention
example1: Short breaks can improve your concentration span while studying.
example1_ru: Короткие перерывы могут улучшить продолжительность концентрации внимания при учёбе.
example2: Long lectures can test even a good concentration span.

en_definition: the act of delaying tasks that need to be done
example1: Procrastination is a common problem among university students.
example1_ru: Прокрастинация — распространённая проблема среди студентов университета.
example2: She overcame procrastination by setting smaller daily goals.

en_definition: a schedule planned for studying before an exam
example1: Make a revision timetable at least two weeks before exams.
example1_ru: Составьте график повторения материала как минимум за две недели до экзаменов.
example2: Sticking to a revision timetable reduces last-minute stress.

en_definition: a small card with information used to help memorize facts
example1: She uses flashcards to memorize new vocabulary every day.
example1_ru: Она каждый день использует карточки для запоминания новых слов.
example2: Flashcards are useful for reviewing key terms before an exam.

en_definition: a technique used to help remember information easily
example1: We use a mnemonic to remember the order of the planets.
example1_ru: Мы используем мнемоническое правило, чтобы запомнить порядок планет.
example2: A good mnemonic makes memorizing formulas much easier.

en_definition: learning by repeating information until it is memorized, without deep understanding
example1: Rote learning helps with vocabulary but not always with understanding grammar.
example1_ru: Механическое заучивание помогает с лексикой, но не всегда с пониманием грамматики.
example2: The new curriculum discourages rote learning in favor of critical thinking.

en_definition: the process of evaluating your own performance or understanding
example1: Complete the self-assessment before the tutorial to identify weak areas.
example1_ru: Заполните форму самооценки перед семинаром, чтобы выявить слабые места.
example2: Regular self-assessment helps track your progress over the semester.

en_definition: a specific skill or piece of knowledge students should gain from a course
example1: Each module lists its intended learning outcomes at the very start.
example1_ru: У каждого модуля результаты обучения перечислены в самом начале.
example2: The exam is designed to test the course's key learning outcomes.

en_definition: ongoing assessment used to monitor learning and provide feedback
example1: Weekly quizzes serve as formative assessment throughout the course.
example1_ru: Еженедельные тесты служат формирующим оцениванием на протяжении курса.
example2: Formative assessment does not usually count toward the final grade.

en_definition: a final evaluation used to measure overall achievement at the end of a course
example1: The final exam is the main summative assessment for this module.
example1_ru: Итоговый экзамен — основное итоговое оценивание для этого модуля.
example2: Summative assessment determines your overall grade for the term.

en_definition: a course that students can complete at their own speed
example1: The self-paced course allows students to finish modules whenever they want.
example1_ru: Курс с индивидуальным темпом позволяет студентам завершать модули в любое удобное время.
example2: Self-paced courses require strong self-discipline to complete on time.

en_definition: a teaching method that combines online and face-to-face learning
example1: The program uses blended learning to combine lectures and online activities.
example1_ru: Программа использует смешанное обучение, сочетая лекции и онлайн-занятия.
example2: Blended learning gives students more flexibility in their studies.

en_definition: learning that does not require students and teachers to be online at the same time
example1: Asynchronous learning lets students watch recorded lectures at any time.
example1_ru: Асинхронное обучение позволяет студентам смотреть записанные лекции в любое время.
example2: The course offers both live sessions and asynchronous learning materials.

en_definition: a seminar or presentation conducted over the internet
example1: Register for the webinar on academic writing skills.
example1_ru: Зарегистрируйтесь на вебинар по навыкам академического письма.
example2: The webinar recording will be shared with all participants.

en_definition: the act of showing your computer screen to others during an online meeting
example1: Use screen-sharing to show your slides during the presentation.
example1_ru: Используйте демонстрацию экрана, чтобы показать слайды во время презентации.
example2: Screen-sharing made it easier to explain the coding problem.

en_definition: a separate online space where a small group can discuss during a video call
example1: Students were split into breakout rooms for the group task.
example1_ru: Студентов разделили на виртуальные комнаты для группового задания.
example2: Return to the main session after ten minutes in the breakout room.

en_definition: an unwanted high-pitched noise caused by sound looping through a microphone
example1: Microphone feedback interrupted the lecturer's presentation several times during the talk.
example1_ru: Писк микрофона несколько раз прерывал выступление лектора во время доклада.
example2: Mute your microphone to avoid feedback during the call.

en_definition: a delay in video or audio playback while data loads
example1: The lecture video kept buffering because of slow internet.
example1_ru: Видео с лекцией постоянно зависало из-за медленного интернета.
example2: Buffering can be reduced by lowering the video quality.

en_definition: a small, temporary problem with a machine or system
example1: A software glitch caused the online exam to freeze.
example1_ru: Программный сбой привёл к зависанию онлайн-экзамена.
example2: The support team fixed the glitch within an hour.

en_definition: to identify and fix problems, especially in a technical system
example1: IT support helped troubleshoot the connection issue within a few minutes.
example1_ru: Служба технической поддержки помогла устранить проблему с подключением за несколько минут.
example2: Try to troubleshoot the printer before calling for help.

en_definition: to restart a computer or device
example1: Reboot your laptop if the software stops responding.
example1_ru: Перезагрузите ноутбук, если программа перестала отвечать.
example2: The technician recommended rebooting the router first.

en_definition: the ability of a device or software to work correctly with another
example1: Check the compatibility of the software before installing it.
example1_ru: Проверьте совместимость программы перед её установкой.
example2: There is a compatibility issue between the two file formats.

en_definition: to arrange for a task to be done by an outside company or person
example1: The university outsourced its dining services to a private company.
example1_ru: Университет передал услуги общественного питания на аутсорсинг частной компании.
example2: Many firms outsource software testing to specialized agencies.

en_definition: to make something easier to understand or do
example1: The new form simplifies the process of requesting a transcript.
example1_ru: Новая форма упрощает процесс запроса академической справки.
example2: Try to simplify your explanation for a general audience.

en_definition: a point in a process that causes delay or slows everything down
example1: The approval step was a major bottleneck in the application process.
example1_ru: Этап утверждения был главным узким местом в процессе подачи заявки.
example2: Identifying the bottleneck helped speed up production.

en_definition: an important stage or event in the progress of something
example1: Submitting the literature review is the first milestone of the thesis.
example1_ru: Сдача обзора литературы — это первая важная веха диссертации.
example2: The team celebrated reaching a major project milestone.

en_definition: a specific item or result that must be produced as part of a project
example1: The final report is the main deliverable for this module.
example1_ru: Итоговый отчёт — главный сдаваемый результат этого модуля.
example2: Each team member is responsible for one project deliverable.

en_definition: a person or group with an interest in a project's outcome
example1: The report must consider the views of all stakeholders.
example1_ru: В отчёте необходимо учитывать мнения всех заинтересованных сторон.
example2: Students, teachers, and parents are stakeholders in school policy.

en_definition: an assessment to check if a plan or project is practical to carry out
example1: The feasibility study showed the project would be too expensive.
example1_ru: Технико-экономическое обоснование показало, что проект будет слишком дорогим.
example2: A feasibility study is required before construction can begin.

en_definition: the process of identifying and evaluating possible dangers or problems
example1: A risk assessment is required before starting any lab experiment.
example1_ru: Перед началом любого лабораторного эксперимента требуется оценка рисков.
example2: The risk assessment identified several safety concerns.

en_definition: a backup plan for use if something goes wrong
example1: Always have a contingency plan in case equipment fails.
example1_ru: Всегда имейте план на случай непредвиденных обстоятельств, если оборудование выйдет из строя.
example2: The team's contingency plan saved the project when the server crashed.

en_definition: clothing or gear used to prevent injury during work or experiments
example1: Wear protective equipment before handling the chemicals in the laboratory.
example1_ru: Надевайте защитное снаряжение перед работой с химикатами в лаборатории.
example2: The lab provides protective equipment for all students.

en_definition: dangerous, especially causing risk of harm
example1: Hazardous chemicals must be stored in a locked cabinet.
example1_ru: Опасные химикаты должны храниться в запертом шкафу.
example2: The sign warned of hazardous conditions ahead.

en_definition: a system for supplying fresh air to a room or space
example1: Good ventilation is essential when working with strong chemicals.
example1_ru: При работе с сильными химикатами необходима хорошая вентиляция.
example2: The laboratory has a special ventilation system for fumes.

en_definition: a ventilated enclosure used to safely handle dangerous fumes
example1: Always mix strong acids inside a fume hood.
example1_ru: Всегда смешивайте сильные кислоты в вытяжном шкафу.
example2: The fume hood removes toxic gases from the lab air.

en_definition: an accidental release of a liquid onto a surface
example1: Report any chemical spillage to the lab technician immediately.
example1_ru: Немедленно сообщайте лаборанту о любом разливе химикатов.
example2: The spillage was cleaned up quickly to prevent slipping.

en_definition: a period of isolation to prevent the spread of disease
example1: Students with symptoms were asked to enter quarantine.
example1_ru: Студентов с симптомами попросили уйти на карантин.
example2: The quarantine period lasted two weeks.

en_definition: able to be spread from one person to another through contact
example1: The flu is highly contagious in crowded dormitories.
example1_ru: Грипп очень заразен в переполненных общежитиях.
example2: Stay home if you have a contagious illness.

en_definition: the body's natural defense against illness and infection
example1: A healthy diet supports a strong immune system.
example1_ru: Здоровое питание поддерживает крепкую иммунную систему.
example2: Lack of sleep can weaken your immune system.

en_definition: continuing for a long time or recurring frequently
example1: Chronic stress can affect both physical and mental health.
example1_ru: Хронический стресс может влиять как на физическое, так и на психическое здоровье.
example2: He suffers from a chronic back problem.

en_definition: sudden and severe, especially about an illness
example1: She was admitted to hospital with acute appendicitis.
example1_ru: Её госпитализировали с острым аппендицитом.
example2: The acute pain disappeared after a few hours.

en_definition: a service offering support and advice for personal or emotional problems
example1: The university offers a free counseling service for stressed students.
example1_ru: Университет предлагает бесплатную службу психологического консультирования для студентов в стрессе.
example2: She booked an appointment with the counseling service.

en_definition: a person's overall mental and emotional health
example1: The workshop focuses on students' mental well-being during exams.
example1_ru: Мастер-класс посвящён психическому благополучию студентов во время экзаменов.
example2: Regular exercise contributes to better mental well-being.

en_definition: a state of not getting enough sleep
example1: Sleep deprivation makes it harder to concentrate on studies.
example1_ru: Недосып затрудняет концентрацию на учёбе.
example2: Long-term sleep deprivation can affect memory and mood.

en_definition: containing substances the body needs to stay healthy
example1: The cafeteria now offers more nutritious meal options.
example1_ru: В столовой теперь предлагают более питательные варианты блюд.
example2: A nutritious breakfast helps you focus during morning classes.

en_definition: involving a lot of sitting and little physical activity
example1: A sedentary lifestyle can lead to health problems over time.
example1_ru: Малоподвижный образ жизни со временем может привести к проблемам со здоровьем.
example2: Long study sessions often make students more sedentary.

en_definition: the position in which someone holds their body
example1: Poor posture while studying can cause back pain.
example1_ru: Плохая осанка во время учёбы может вызвать боль в спине.
example2: Try to maintain good posture at your desk.

en_definition: tiredness or discomfort in the eyes from prolonged use, such as screen time
example1: Take regular breaks to avoid eye strain from the computer screen.
example1_ru: Делайте регулярные перерывы, чтобы избежать усталости глаз от экрана компьютера.
example2: Eye strain can cause headaches after long study sessions.

en_definition: an organized set of activities designed to improve health and well-being
example1: The university launched a wellness program for stressed students.
example1_ru: Университет запустил программу заботы о здоровье для студентов в стрессе.
example2: The wellness program includes yoga and meditation sessions.

en_definition: help and encouragement given by people of similar age or status
example1: Peer support groups help students adjust to university life.
example1_ru: Группы поддержки сверстников помогают студентам адаптироваться к университетской жизни.
example2: She found peer support helpful during her first semester.

en_definition: difficult or unpleasant conditions in life
example1: The scholarship was created to help students facing financial hardship.
example1_ru: Стипендия была создана для помощи студентам, испытывающим финансовые трудности.
example2: Many families faced hardship during the economic downturn.

en_definition: to manage or deal with pressure and difficulty
example1: Exercise is one healthy way to cope with stress.
example1_ru: Физические упражнения — один из здоровых способов справляться со стрессом.
example2: The workshop taught useful techniques to cope with stress.

en_definition: to relax after a period of work or stress
example1: She likes to unwind by taking a walk after class.
example1_ru: Она любит расслабляться, гуляя после занятий.
example2: It's important to unwind before exams to avoid burnout.

en_definition: to rest in order to regain energy
example1: A weekend away helped her recharge before final exams.
example1_ru: Выходные вдали от учёбы помогли ей восстановить силы перед выпускными экзаменами.
example2: Take time to recharge between long study sessions.

en_definition: the practice of focusing calmly on the present moment
example1: The counseling center offers a weekly mindfulness session.
example1_ru: Консультационный центр проводит еженедельные занятия по осознанности.
example2: Mindfulness exercises can reduce anxiety before exams.

en_definition: influence from people of the same age to behave in a certain way
example1: Peer pressure can make it hard to say no to social events during exam week.
example1_ru: Давление сверстников может затруднить отказ от общественных мероприятий во время сессии.
example2: She learned to resist peer pressure and study on her own schedule.

en_definition: a feeling of being accepted and part of a group or community
example1: Joining a club can give new students a sense of belonging.
example1_ru: Вступление в клуб может дать новым студентам чувство принадлежности к сообществу.
example2: The orientation program aims to build a sense of belonging early on.

en_definition: the process of getting used to a new and different culture
example1: Cultural adjustment can take several months for international students.
example1_ru: Культурная адаптация может занять несколько месяцев у иностранных студентов.
example2: The buddy program helps ease cultural adjustment for newcomers.
